nsf.name: National Science Foundation (NSF)

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Must contain: (1) a statement of the project activity, (2) an explicit statement of the intellectual merit, and (3) a statement of the broader impacts. Separate these three parts clearly.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Describe the research plan including: (1) objectives, (2) methods, (3) expected significance, (4) relation to current knowledge, (5) plan for work, (6) results from prior NSF support. Include sufficient detail for review.

required_sections.name: References Cited
required_sections.guidance: Each reference must include: author(s), publication title, book title, journal volume, page numbers, year. Use NSF bibliography format. Include only relevant citations.

required_sections.name: Biographical Sketches
required_sections.guidance: For each senior personnel: (1) professional preparation, (2) appointments, (3) publications (max 5 relevant), (4) synergistic activities. Use NSF template format.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete all budget categories. Justify all senior personnel time. Include fringe benefits, equipment, travel, participant support, other direct costs, and indirect costs.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Explain each budget category: (1) senior personnel - role and effort, (2) other personnel - duties, (3) fringe benefits, (4) equipment - why needed, (5) travel - purpose and destination, (6) other direct costs, (7) indirect costs - rate and base.

required_sections.name: Current and Pending Support
required_sections.guidance: List all current and pending funding for all senior personnel. Include: funding source, title, amount, period, and relationship to this proposal.

required_sections.name: Facilities and Other Resources
required_sections.guidance: Describe available facilities: (1) institutional support, (2) equipment, (3) office space, (4) computing resources, (5) student housing (if applicable). Explain why institution can do this work.

required_sections.name: Data Management and Sharing Plan
required_sections.guidance: Must include: (1) types of data produced, (2) standards used, (3) data access/sharing, (4) archiving and preservation. Show how you will make data available to others.

required_sections.name: Postdoctoral Mentoring Plan
required_sections.guidance: If postdocs supported, describe: (1) mentoring activities, (2) career development, (3) training in responsible research. Must be separate from project description.

required_documents.name: NSF Cover Sheet
required_documents.description: Cover sheet generated within Research.gov including program announcement, proposal title, PI info, and international cooperation boxes.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate NSF Cover Sheet data from organization profile, project info, and PI information

required_documents.name: Biographical Sketches (SciENcv format)
required_documents.description: Biographical sketch for each senior/key person. MUST be prepared via SciENcv as of May 2024. Separate sketch per person.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload biographical sketches prepared via SciENcv (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sciencv/). One PDF per senior/key person.

required_documents.name: Synergistic Activities
required_documents.description: Lists up to 5 examples of synergistic activities per senior/key person. Separated from biographical sketch as of May 2024.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload Synergistic Activities document for each senior/key person. Up to 5 examples per person. Separate from biosketch.

required_documents.name: Collaborators and Other Affiliations (COA)
required_documents.description: Required per PAPPG for each senior/key person. Must use NSF COA template.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload Collaborators and Other Affiliations using the NSF COA template for each senior/key person.

required_documents.name: Current and Pending (Other) Support (SciENcv)
required_documents.description: Current and pending support for each senior/key person. MUST be prepared via SciENcv. PDF uploaded to Research.gov.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload Current and Pending Support prepared via SciENcv for each senior/key person.

required_documents.name: Data Management and Sharing Plan
required_documents.description: Plan describing data types produced, standards used, access/sharing provisions, and archiving/preservation strategy. Max 2 pages.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a Data Management and Sharing Plan describing data types, standards, access/sharing, and preservation (max 2 pages)

required_documents.name: Mentoring Plan
required_documents.description: Required if postdocs or graduate students are supported. Describes mentoring activities, career development, and responsible research training. Max 1 page.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a Mentoring Plan for postdoctoral researchers and/or graduate students (max 1 page)

required_documents.name: Letters of Collaboration
required_documents.description: Letters from partner organizations confirming their role. Must use NSF-approved format with brief, factual statements only.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of collaboration in NSF-approved format from a partner organization

required_documents.name: IRS 501(c)(3) Determination Letter
required_documents.description: Proof of tax-exempt status for nonprofit applicants.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your IRS determination letter (PDF)

required_documents.name: Board Resolution
required_documents.description: Board authorization to apply for this grant.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a board resolution authorizing the grant application

required_documents.name: Most Recent Audit Report
required_documents.description: Independent financial audit or Single Audit (if >$1M federal expenditures per year).
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your most recent independent audit (PDF)

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: NSF ID
prerequisites.description: Unique alphanumeric identifier for individual PIs via Research.gov.

prerequisites.name: Research.gov Profile
prerequisites.description: Organizational profile in Research.gov required for submission.

prerequisites.name: Authorized Organizational Representative (AOR)
prerequisites.description: Designated representative authorized to submit proposals on behalf of the organization.

davis_bacon.description: Requires paying prevailing wages on federally-funded construction projects over $2,000. NSF rarely funds construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement to provide employment and contracting opportunities to low-income residents. Not applicable to NSF.

nepa.description: National Environmental Policy Act review required for projects affecting the physical environment. Rarely applies to NSF except Antarctic research or certain field work.

buy_america.description: Requires using American-made iron, steel, and manufactured products in infrastructure projects. Not applicable to NSF.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit per 2 CFR 200 Subpart F.

irb.description: Institutional Review Board approval required for research involving human subjects per 45 CFR Part 46.

iacuc.description: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee approval for research involving vertebrate animals.

malign_foreign_talent.description: Senior/key personnel must not be party to a Malign Foreign Talent Recruitment Program (effective May 2024).

data_management_plan.description: All proposals must include a Data Management Plan (max 2 pages) describing how data will be managed, shared, and preserved.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: NSF accepts full negotiated NICRA with no statutory cap. De minimis rate of 15% available for organizations without a negotiated rate.

submission_portal.name: Research.gov
submission_portal.notes: FastLane being phased out; Research.gov is primary submission system. Biosketches must use SciENcv format.

nsf.ai_context: NSF proposals must clearly address Intellectual Merit and Broader Impacts as the two foundational review criteria. The Project Summary must contain three labeled sections: Overview, Intellectual Merit, and Broader Impacts. The PAPPG (currently NSF 24-1) governs all proposal requirements. Nearly all NSF funding is competitive with peer review. Biosketches must use SciENcv format. Senior personnel must certify non-participation in Malign Foreign Talent Recruitment Programs.

doe.name: Department of Energy (DOE)

required_sections.name: Project Abstract
required_sections.guidance: One page summary including: project objectives, methods, expected outcomes, relevance to DOE mission. Must be suitable for public release.

required_sections.name: Project Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Describe: (1) significance/objectives, (2) approach/methods, (3) innovation, (4) timeline/milestones, (5) deliverables. Address DOE policy priorities if applicable.

required_sections.name: Biographical Sketches
required_sections.guidance: Include for key personnel: education, experience, publications, relevant achievements. Use CV format.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Detail all costs by category: personnel, equipment, travel, supplies, subcontracts. Explain why each is necessary for project success.

required_sections.name: Facilities
required_sections.guidance: Describe facilities available for project: lab space, equipment, computing resources, support staff.

required_sections.name: Letters of Support
required_sections.guidance: Include letters from collaborators, partners, resource providers. Should explicitly state commitment to project.

required_sections.name: Data Management Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe how data will be managed: collection, storage, sharing, preservation. Include data types, standards, access provisions.

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from collaborators and resource providers confirming commitment
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support from a collaborator for a DOE grant

required_documents.name: Environmental Questionnaire
required_documents.description: DOE environmental compliance questionnaire (NEPA)
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and upload DOE environmental questionnaire

required_documents.name: Intellectual Property Management Plan
required_documents.description: Plan for managing intellectual property developed under the project
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft an intellectual property management plan for a DOE-funded project

required_documents.name: Cost Share Commitment Letter
required_documents.description: Letter documenting cost share commitments from partners
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a cost share commitment letter from a project partner

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

prerequisites.name: ARPA-E eXCHANGE
prerequisites.description: Required for ARPA-E programs only. Register at arpa-e-foa.energy.gov.

prerequisites.name: PAMS Registration
prerequisites.description: DOE Portfolio Analysis and Management System for Office of Science programs.

davis_bacon.description: Requires paying prevailing wages on federally-funded construction projects, especially those funded under IIJA/BIL. Semiannual payroll reports due April 21 and October 21.

section_3.description: HUD requirement to provide employment and contracting opportunities to low-income residents. Not applicable to DOE.

nepa.description: Environmental review required per 10 CFR Part 1021. Many smaller projects qualify for categorical exclusions. DOE streamlined procedures in 2025-2026.

buy_america.description: Build America, Buy America Act (BABA) requires domestically produced iron, steel, manufactured products, and construction materials for infrastructure projects. Applies to FOAs issued after May 14, 2022.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit. DOE uses 2 CFR 910 for for-profit entities.

irb.description: Institutional Review Board approval required for research involving human subjects.

iacuc.description: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee approval for research involving animals.

matching.description: Varies by program and FOA. Check specific NOFO for cost-sharing requirements.

technology_licensing.description: Bayh-Dole Act applies. Recipients retain title to inventions but must disclose and grant government a license. U.S. manufacturing preference.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: DOE accepts negotiated NICRA or de minimis 15%. For-profit entities follow 2 CFR 910 (parallel to 2 CFR 200 for nonprofits).

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov / DOE Exchange Portals
submission_portal.notes: ARPA-E uses eXCHANGE portal (arpa-e-foa.energy.gov). EERE uses EERE Exchange. Office of Science uses PAMS.

doe.ai_context: DOE proposals must address relevance to DOE mission areas (energy, environment, nuclear security). NEPA compliance is critical -- include environmental questionnaire. Buy America applies to infrastructure projects. For ARPA-E, emphasize transformative potential and technology-to-market pathway. DOE has specific cost principles for for-profit entities under 2 CFR 910. Davis-Bacon applies to construction projects, especially under IIJA/BIL funding.

nih.name: National Institutes of Health (NIH)

required_sections.name: Specific Aims
required_sections.guidance: List 3-4 specific aims. Each should be a concrete, measurable step toward the overarching goal. State what you intend to do, not how.

required_sections.name: Research Strategy
required_sections.guidance: Must address: (1) Significance - importance of the problem, (2) Innovation - new approaches, (3) Approach - methods, analysis, interpretation. Organize with clear headings.

required_sections.name: Biographical Sketch
required_sections.guidance: Use NIH format with personal statement, positions/employment, education, peer-reviewed publications (selected), other grant support. Include eRA Commons IDs for senior personnel.

required_sections.name: Budget and Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Use PHS398 budget forms. Justify all personnel costs, explain effort percentages. Include detailed justification for equipment, travel, and other costs.

required_sections.name: Resources and Environment
required_sections.guidance: Describe: institutional resources, equipment available, key personnel expertise, how environment supports the project.

required_sections.name: Resource Sharing Plan
required_sections.guidance: Address: (1) data sharing, (2) model organism sharing, (3) unique research resources. State how you will make these available.

required_sections.name: Vertebrate Animals
required_sections.guidance: If using animals: describe procedures, housing, monitoring, euthanasia. Address pain management, species justification, reduction principles.

required_sections.name: Human Subjects
required_sections.guidance: If using humans: describe recruitment, consent procedures, risk minimization, data protection. Include IRB plan if available.

required_documents.name: IRB Approval or Exemption
required_documents.description: Institutional Review Board approval for human subjects research
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your IRB approval letter or exemption determination

required_documents.name: IACUC Approval
required_documents.description: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee approval for animal research
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your IACUC approval letter

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from collaborators, consultants, and resource providers
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support for an NIH research grant application

required_documents.name: Consortium/Contractual Agreement
required_documents.description: Agreements with subcontracting organizations
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a consortium agreement for an NIH multi-site research project

required_documents.name: Authentication of Key Biological Resources
required_documents.description: Plan for authenticating cell lines, antibodies, and other biological resources
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft an authentication plan for key biological resources used in the project

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: eRA Commons Account
prerequisites.description: Both organizational and individual PI eRA Commons accounts required. PI needs eRA Commons username.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

prerequisites.name: eRA Commons Institutional Profile
prerequisites.description: Organization must have an Institutional Profile registered in eRA Commons.

davis_bacon.description: Requires paying prevailing wages on federally-funded construction projects over $2,000. NIH rarely funds construction.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to NIH.

nepa.description: NIH research typically does not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not applicable to NIH research grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required before human subjects research begins. Multi-site studies must use single IRB per 45 CFR Part 46.

iacuc.description: IACUC approval required; must be granted within 3 years of budget period start date per PHS Policy and Animal Welfare Act.

fcoi.description: Financial Conflict of Interest disclosure required per 42 CFR 50 Subpart F. Investigators must disclose significant financial interests.

data_sharing.description: NIH Data Management and Sharing Plan required with application (effective January 25, 2023). Becomes a condition of award.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: NIH historically accepts full negotiated NICRA. NIH attempted 15% cap in Feb 2025 (NOT-OD-25-068); permanently enjoined by court Apr 2025. Appeal pending as of Mar 2026. Negotiated rates currently in effect.

submission_portal.name: eRA Commons / ASSIST
submission_portal.notes: Applications submitted via ASSIST or Grants.gov, managed through eRA Commons. Biosketches must use SciENcv format.

nih.ai_context: NIH proposals must address Significance, Innovation, and Approach in the Research Strategy section. Specific Aims page (1 page) is the most critical component. Use PHS398 budget forms. Biosketches must use NIH SciENcv format. IRB approval required for human subjects; IACUC for animal research. Financial Conflict of Interest disclosure mandatory. Data Management and Sharing Plan required since Jan 2023. NIH indirect cost rate cap controversy: court blocked 15% cap in Apr 2025, appeal pending.

usda.name: USDA (Rural Development / SBIR)

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Brief summary of project: objectives, methods, expected outcomes. Include relevance to USDA priorities.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) problem statement, (2) objectives, (3) methodology, (4) expected outcomes, (5) relevance to agriculture/rural development.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete all budget categories. Justify all costs. USDA has specific allowability rules - check eligible costs.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Provide detailed justification for each line item. Explain how costs relate to project objectives.

required_sections.name: Organizational Information
required_sections.guidance: Describe: organization history, mission, relevant experience, staff capabilities, governance structure.

required_sections.name: Letters of Support
required_sections.guidance: Include any letters showing partnerships, market interest, or technical support.

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters showing partnerships, market interest, or technical support
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support for a USDA grant application

required_documents.name: IRS 501(c)(3) Determination Letter
required_documents.description: Proof of tax-exempt status (if nonprofit applicant)
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your IRS determination letter (PDF)

required_documents.name: USDA Assurance Statements
required_documents.description: Required certifications and assurances for USDA grants
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and upload USDA assurance statements

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

davis_bacon.description: Applies to construction projects in some Rural Development programs. Must pay workers the prevailing local wage.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to USDA.

nepa.description: Environmental review required per 7 CFR Part 799 for projects with potential environmental impact.

buy_america.description: BABA applies to infrastructure-related programs. American-made materials may be required.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: IRB approval required for research involving human subjects.

iacuc.description: IACUC approval for research involving animals.

matching.description: Varies by program. Check specific NOFO.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: USDA NIFA programs have a statutory 30% cap on indirect costs (30% of total federal funds). This is set by Congress and cannot be waived. De minimis rate of 15% also available.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: NIFA uses specific application systems for some programs. SBIR applications may have separate submission requirements.

usda.ai_context: USDA proposals should emphasize relevance to agriculture, food systems, or rural development. NIFA research grants have a unique 30% statutory cap on indirect costs. Land-grant institutions (1862, 1890, 1994) have priority for many NIFA programs. Environmental review (NEPA) applies. Civil rights compliance is actively audited by USDA. Rural Development programs generally serve communities under 50,000 population.

epa.name: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency)

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Brief overview: what, why, how, expected results. Include environmental outcomes.

required_sections.name: Project Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) statement of work, (2) quality assurance if applicable, (3) timeout milestones, (4) environmental outcomes, (5) transfer/promotion plan.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed budget with justifications. EPA has specific cost principles - follow EPA guidance.

required_sections.name: Environmental Compliance
required_sections.guidance: If project may have environmental impacts, describe compliance with all applicable environmental laws.

required_documents.name: EPA 4700-4 Preaward Compliance Review Report
required_documents.description: Civil rights preaward review form. Must be completely filled out or EPA will follow up. Version 5.0 current.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Complete and upload EPA Form 4700-4 Preaward Compliance Review Report. All fields must be filled or EPA will require follow-up.

required_documents.name: EPA 5700-54 Key Contacts Form
required_documents.description: Lists key project contacts including PI, project officer, and additional contacts.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate EPA 5700-54 Key Contacts Form from project contact information

required_documents.name: QAPP Commitment Letter
required_documents.description: Commitment to develop a Quality Assurance Project Plan within 30-90 days after award for projects collecting environmental data. Cannot begin data collection until EPA approves QAPP.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a QAPP commitment letter stating the organization will develop a Quality Assurance Project Plan per EPA QA/R-5 requirements within 90 days of award

required_documents.name: Budget Narrative
required_documents.description: Detailed justification for each budget category explaining why costs are reasonable, necessary, and allowable.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate a budget narrative justifying each budget category for an EPA grant application

required_documents.name: Quality Assurance Project Plan (QAPP)
required_documents.description: Full quality assurance plan for environmental data collection. Typically required post-award within 30-90 days.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a Quality Assurance Project Plan outline for an EPA grant per QA/R-5 requirements

required_documents.name: Environmental Permits
required_documents.description: Any existing environmental permits relevant to the project.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload copies of any relevant environmental permits

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

davis_bacon.description: Applies to construction-related grants (water infrastructure, brownfields cleanup with construction). Must pay workers the prevailing local wage.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to EPA.

nepa.description: Environmental review may be required depending on scope of activities, even for an environmental agency.

buy_america.description: BABA applies to EPA infrastructure grants. American-made iron, steel, and construction materials may be required.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not typically applicable to EPA grants.

iacuc.description: Not typically applicable to EPA grants.

matching.description: Varies by program. Check specific NOFO.

quality_assurance.description: Recipients collecting environmental data must have an approved Quality Management Plan (QMP) and/or Quality Assurance Project Plan (QAPP) per EPA QA/R-2 and QA/R-5.

mbe_wbe.description: Must make good faith efforts to award contracts to Minority Business Enterprises and Women Business Enterprises per 40 CFR Part 33. EPA Form 5700-52A required.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: EPA accepts negotiated NICRA or de minimis 15%. No EPA-specific statutory cap on indirect costs.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: EPA also has its own Grants Management portal for post-award management.

epa.ai_context: EPA proposals must clearly describe environmental outcomes and benefits. Quality Assurance Project Plans (QAPPs) are required for any environmental data collection, typically due 30-90 days after award. MBE/WBE utilization targets must be addressed. Brownfields programs require state environmental authority letter of acknowledgment. Environmental Justice grants should address disproportionate environmental impacts on underserved communities. Title VI compliance is especially important for EJ programs.

dot.name: DOT (Department of Transportation)

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Overview of project including: title, location, description, funding amount requested, match amount.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) problem/opportunity, (2) project goals, (3) scope of work, (4) methodology, (5) timeline, (6) expected outcomes, (7) how addresses DOT priorities.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed line-item budget. DOT often requires cost sharing/matching - document clearly.

required_sections.name: Letters of Support
required_sections.guidance: Include letters from partners, local government support, stakeholders.

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from local government, partners, and stakeholders
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support from a local government official for a DOT grant

required_documents.name: Cost Share/Match Documentation
required_documents.description: Documentation of matching funds or cost share commitments
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a cost share commitment letter for a DOT grant application

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with AOR for discretionary grants.

prerequisites.name: TrAMS/FMIS Access
prerequisites.description: FTA uses TrAMS (Transit Award Management System); FHWA uses FMIS for post-award management.

davis_bacon.description: Applies to all federally funded construction contracts over $2,000. Weekly certified payroll reports (WH-347) required. Must post wage determination at job site.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to DOT.

nepa.description: Required for all federally funded transportation projects involving construction or physical changes per 23 CFR 771. Process can take months (CE) to years (EIS). Section 4(f) protections for parks and historic sites.

buy_america.description: ALL iron and steel must be produced in the US (melted and poured domestically). Manufactured products and construction materials must also be domestically produced per BABAA. Waivers available but difficult to obtain.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not typically applicable to DOT grants.

iacuc.description: Not typically applicable to DOT grants.

matching.description: Typically 80% federal / 20% local match. Rural and equity areas may qualify for up to 100% federal. FTA operating: 50/50. Interstate: 90/10.

dbe.description: Disadvantaged Business Enterprise requirements per 49 CFR Part 26. Recipients must set overall DBE goals updated every 3 years. DBE firms must be UCP-certified.

ada.description: All transportation facilities and services must be ADA-accessible. New construction must be fully accessible.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: DOT follows 2 CFR Part 1201. State DOTs must have indirect cost plans approved by FHWA. Unrecovered indirect costs may be used for match with prior DOT approval.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: DOT has a DOT Navigator web resource to help first-time applicants. BUILD/RAISE grants submit through Grants.gov.

dot.ai_context: DOT proposals must address merit criteria including safety, economic competitiveness, quality of life, environmental sustainability, innovation, and state of good repair. Benefit-Cost Analysis (BCA) is required for BUILD/RAISE/INFRA grants using DOT methodology and 3.1% discount rate. Davis-Bacon and Buy America are strictly enforced. NEPA review must be completed before construction funds are obligated. DBE utilization goals required. Match requirements vary: 80/20 standard, up to 100% federal for rural and equity areas. BUILD grants were rebranded from RAISE in FY2026.

nist.name: NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology)
nist.full_name: National Institute of Standards and Technology

required_sections.name: Technical Proposal
required_sections.guidance: Include: (1) technical objectives, (2) approach/methodology, (3) innovation, (4) work plan, (5) deliverables, (6) relevance to NIST mission.

required_sections.name: Management Proposal
required_sections.guidance: Describe: project management approach, timeline/milestones, personnel qualifications, risk management.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Detailed budget with justifications. NIST often supports equipment purchases.

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from industry partners and collaborators
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support from an industry partner for a NIST grant

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Registration on Grants.gov with Authorized Organization Representative (AOR).

prerequisites.name: SBA Company Registry
prerequisites.description: Required for SBIR/STTR only. Register at SBIR.gov with annual certification.

davis_bacon.description: NIST rarely funds construction projects.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to NIST.

nepa.description: NIST grants generally do not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not typically applicable to NIST grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not typically applicable to NIST grants.

iacuc.description: Not typically applicable to NIST grants.

matching.description: MEP program requires minimum 1:1 cost share (50% federal, 50% non-federal). SBIR/STTR has NO matching requirement. Research grants vary by program.

bayh_dole.description: Recipient retains title to inventions but must disclose to NIST within 2 months, elect title within 2 years, and file patent within 1 year. Government gets royalty-free license. NIST is the lead agency interpreting Bayh-Dole march-in rights.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: NIST follows 2 CFR 200. SBIR Phase I: up to $100,000 total (inclusive of indirect). Phase II: up to $400,000 total. High indirect rates reduce money available for actual work.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: NIST is a relatively small granting agency. MEP and SBIR are the primary external grant programs.

nist.ai_context: NIST proposals must connect to standards, measurement, or manufacturing missions. Bayh-Dole IP provisions are critical -- NIST is the lead agency on march-in rights interpretation. MEP Centers require 1:1 matching and use NIST MEP performance measurement system. SBIR awards are among the smallest across agencies (Phase I: $100K). Cybersecurity grants may require alignment with NIST Cybersecurity Framework. NIST provides substantial involvement through cooperative agreements, not just funding.

generic.name: Generic Federal Grant

required_sections.name: Project Summary/Abstract
required_sections.guidance: Brief overview of project: what, why, how, expected results. Make it compelling in one page.

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Full narrative including: statement of need, project objectives, methods/approach, expected outcomes, evaluation plan, sustainability.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Complete budget forms with all categories. Ensure costs are allowable, reasonable, and allocable.

required_sections.name: Budget Justification
required_sections.guidance: Explain each budget category: why needed, how calculated, how relates to project.

required_sections.name: Key Personnel
required_sections.guidance: Describe qualifications of key staff: education, experience, relevant expertise, role in project.

required_sections.name: Facilities and Resources
required_sections.guidance: Describe available resources: equipment, space, staff, institutional support.

required_sections.name: Evaluation Plan
required_sections.guidance: How will you measure success? Include metrics, data collection methods, analysis plan.

required_sections.name: Sustainability Plan
required_sections.guidance: How will project continue after grant ends? Include other funding sources, revenue generation, institutional support.

required_documents.description: Core application form required for virtually all federal grants. Version 4.0 current.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-424 Application for Federal Assistance from organization profile, project info, and contact data

required_documents.description: Standard assurances for non-construction programs. Requires authorized representative signature.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Download SF-424B from Grants.gov, have your Authorized Representative sign it, and upload the signed form.

required_documents.name: SF-LLL Disclosure of Lobbying Activities
required_documents.description: Required if award exceeds $100,000. Discloses any paid lobbying activities. Submit with 'N/A' if no lobbying.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Generate SF-LLL Disclosure of Lobbying Activities form

required_documents.name: Indirect Cost Rate Documentation
required_documents.description: Federally-approved NICRA or de minimis 15% rate certification per 2 CFR 200.414(f). De minimis only if org has never had a negotiated rate.
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your current federally-approved Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA), or certify use of the 15% de minimis rate.

required_documents.name: IRS 501(c)(3) Determination Letter
required_documents.description: Proof of tax-exempt status (if nonprofit applicant).
required_documents.upload_instructions: Upload your IRS determination letter (PDF)

required_documents.name: Letters of Support
required_documents.description: Letters from collaborators, partners, or stakeholders confirming their commitment to the project.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a letter of support for a federal grant application

required_documents.name: Board Resolution
required_documents.description: Board authorization to apply for this grant.
required_documents.generate_prompt: Draft a board resolution authorizing the grant application

section_3.description: HUD-specific requirement. Check if HUD funds are involved.

nepa.description: National Environmental Policy Act review may be required depending on program. Check specific NOFO.

buy_america.description: May apply to infrastructure projects. Check specific NOFO.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Required only for research involving human subjects.

iacuc.description: Required only for research involving animals.

matching.description: Varies by program. Check specific NOFO for cost-sharing requirements.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: De minimis rate of 15% available for organizations without a negotiated rate per 2024 Uniform Guidance revision (effective October 1, 2024).

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: Some agencies use additional portals. Check the specific NOFO for submission instructions.

generic.ai_context: This is a generic federal grant template. When generating content, follow standard federal grant writing conventions: clear statement of need with data, measurable objectives, detailed methodology, realistic budget, and strong evaluation plan. Ensure all costs are allowable, reasonable, and allocable per 2 CFR 200. Address all selection criteria from the NOFO in the order presented.

nea.name: NEA - Grants for Arts Projects

prerequisites.name: SAM.gov Registration
prerequisites.description: Active registration at SAM.gov is required for all federal grants.

prerequisites.name: Unique Entity Identifier (UEI)
prerequisites.description: 12-character identifier from SAM.gov, replaced DUNS in April 2022.

prerequisites.name: Grants.gov Account
prerequisites.description: Applications submitted through Grants.gov.

prerequisites.name: IRS 501(c)(3) Determination Letter
prerequisites.description: Proof of nonprofit status required. Government entities exempt.

prerequisites.name: 3-Year Programming History
prerequisites.description: Must demonstrate at least 3 years (5 years for FY27+) of arts programming prior to application deadline.

required_sections.name: Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Describe your project in 6000 characters or less. Include: what you will do, who will benefit, why this matters, how you will evaluate success.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Provide a line-item budget. Include: personnel, supplies, travel, other costs. Show your total project cost and amount requested.

required_sections.name: Work Sample
required_sections.guidance: Submit 6-10 examples of your recent work. For artists: images, video, audio. For organizations: programs, events, productions.

required_sections.name: Artist/Staff Biographies
required_sections.guidance: Brief bios (100 words each) of key artists or staff who will work on this project.

required_sections.name: Letters of Support
required_sections.guidance: Include 2-3 letters from partners, collaborators, or community members who support this project.

davis_bacon.description: NEA does not fund construction projects.

section_3.description: HUD requirement. Not applicable to NEA.

nepa.description: NEA grants do not trigger NEPA review.

buy_america.description: Not applicable to NEA grants.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit.

irb.description: Not applicable to NEA grants.

matching.description: All NEA grants require minimum 1:1 match from non-federal sources. Federal funds cannot be used as match. In-kind contributions valued at fair market value are acceptable.

no_subgranting.description: Congressional restriction prohibits regranting NEA funds to other organizations or individuals. Exceptions: state arts agencies, regional arts organizations, designated local arts agencies. Subcontracting for project services IS allowed.

accessibility_ada.description: All NEA-funded projects must be accessible to people with disabilities -- physically and programmatically. Must provide accessibility plan in application.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: NEA follows 2 CFR 200. Grants are typically small ($10,000-$100,000). High indirect rates can consume a large portion of the grant and may be viewed unfavorably by reviewers.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: NEA applications submitted through Grants.gov. Two annual deadlines (typically February and July). Late applications rejected without review.

nea.ai_context: NEA proposals emphasize artistic quality above all other criteria -- unlike most federal grants. Work samples are critical and must demonstrate artistic merit. All grants require 1:1 non-federal match. No subgranting allowed (cannot regrant funds). Accessibility/ADA compliance must be explicitly addressed. Content restrictions apply (no obscenity per Miller test). NEA grants are small ($10K-$100K) but carry full federal compliance burden. NEA acknowledgment and logo required in all published materials.

nea_challenge.name: NEA - Challenge America

prerequisites.description: Primarily targets organizations with budgets under $250,000.

required_sections.name: Project Summary
required_sections.guidance: Brief description: what, who benefits, why important. 2-3 sentences.

required_sections.name: Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Describe: (1) artistic merit, (2) reaching new audiences, (3) how project benefits underserved community. Be concise.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Simple budget. Request is fixed at $10,000. Show how funds will be used.

required_sections.name: Audience Development
required_sections.guidance: Describe who your current audience is and how this project will reach NEW audiences - people who don't typically attend your programs.

davis_bacon.description: Not applicable.

section_3.description: Not applicable.

nepa.description: Not applicable.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Organizations spending $1M+ in federal funds per year must have a Single Audit. Rarely triggered given small grant size.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Fixed $10,000 grant requires $10,000 non-federal match. Total project budget must be at least $20,000.

no_subgranting.description: Cannot regrant NEA funds to other organizations.

accessibility_ada.description: Events must be in accessible venues with program access for people with disabilities.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Grant is fixed at $10,000. Indirect costs would significantly reduce direct project spending. Most Challenge America grantees do not claim indirect.

submission_portal.name: Grants.gov
submission_portal.notes: Challenge America was folded into Grants for Arts Projects as a discipline for FY2026.

nea_challenge.ai_context: Challenge America targets small organizations (budgets under $250,000), first-time NEA applicants, and underserved communities. Grant is fixed at $10,000 with required 1:1 match. Narrative is shorter than standard GAP (4,000 characters). Focus on reaching new audiences and underserved populations. Artistic merit and community impact are key criteria. Now operates within the Grants for Arts Projects framework as of FY2026.

artist_individual.name: Artist Individual Grant

required_sections.name: Artist Statement
required_sections.guidance: Briefly describe your work as an artist. What do you create? Why? What makes your approach unique?

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Describe your proposed project: what you will create, process, timeline, expected outcome.

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Show how you will use the grant funds. Include materials, equipment, workspace, other expenses.

required_sections.name: Work Samples
required_sections.guidance: Submit 5-10 examples of your recent work that relate to this project.

required_sections.name: Artist Biography
required_sections.guidance: Brief bio: your background, training, exhibitions, awards. Help reviewers understand your experience.

davis_bacon.description: Not applicable to individual artist grants.

section_3.description: Not applicable.

nepa.description: Not applicable.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Not applicable to individual grants from non-federal sources.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Varies by program. Some require matching, most do not for individual artists.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Individual artist grants typically do not allow indirect cost claims. All funds go directly to the artist/project.

submission_portal.name: Various
submission_portal.notes: State arts councils, private foundations, and individual artist programs each have their own submission systems.

artist_individual.ai_context: Individual artist grants prioritize artistic quality and creative vision. Work samples are the most important component. Artist statement should convey unique voice and approach. Budget should be straightforward listing of project expenses. No nonprofit status required for most programs. These are typically from state arts councils or private foundations, not federal agencies (except NEA Literature Fellowships and Jazz Masters).

micro_grant.name: Micro-Grant (Under $5K)

required_sections.name: Your Idea
required_sections.guidance: What do you want to do? Describe your project in plain language. What will you create or accomplish?

required_sections.name: Why It Matters
required_sections.guidance: Why is this project important? Who will benefit? What impact will it have?

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: How will you use the grant? List main expenses. Keep it simple.

required_sections.name: About You
required_sections.guidance: Briefly: who are you? Why are you the right person to do this? Any relevant experience?

davis_bacon.description: Not applicable to micro-grants.

section_3.description: Not applicable.

nepa.description: Not applicable.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Not applicable. Micro-grants are typically from non-federal sources.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Most micro-grants do not require matching funds.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Micro-grants under $5K typically do not allow indirect cost claims. All funds go directly to the project.

submission_portal.name: Various
submission_portal.notes: Awesome Foundation, local community foundations, and other micro-grant programs each have their own simple application processes.

micro_grant.ai_context: Micro-grants (under $5K) have minimal compliance requirements and simple applications. Focus on a compelling idea, clear impact, and realistic budget. Keep language plain and accessible. No jargon needed. Most programs value creativity, community impact, and feasibility over organizational capacity.

small_business_grant.name: Small Business Grant

required_sections.name: Business Description
required_sections.guidance: Describe your business: what you do, your target customers, what makes you unique, your experience.

required_sections.name: Project Proposal
required_sections.guidance: What specifically will you use this grant for? Describe your project or growth plan in detail.

required_sections.name: Use of Funds
required_sections.guidance: Line-item budget showing exactly how you will spend the grant money. Be specific.

required_sections.name: Expected Impact
required_sections.guidance: What will this grant help you achieve? Include metrics if possible: revenue, customers, jobs created.

required_sections.name: Financial Information
required_sections.guidance: Basic financial info: revenue, expenses, any existing funding. Keep it brief.

davis_bacon.description: Not typically applicable to small business grants.

section_3.description: Not applicable.

nepa.description: Not applicable.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Not applicable for most state/private small business grants.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Varies by program. Some require matching funds or co-investment.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Small business grants from state programs and private competitions typically do not use indirect cost rate structures. Budget is project-based.

submission_portal.name: Various
submission_portal.notes: State programs, private competitions, and SBA-affiliated programs each have their own portals.

small_business_grant.ai_context: Small business grants emphasize business viability, market opportunity, job creation, and economic impact. Include concrete metrics (revenue projections, jobs created, customers served). Show clear use of funds tied to growth. Demonstrate market demand and competitive advantage. Financial information should show business sustainability beyond the grant period.

community_project.name: Community Project Grant

required_sections.name: Statement of Need
required_sections.guidance: Describe the community need your project addresses. Use data if available. Why is this important NOW?

required_sections.name: Project Description
required_sections.guidance: Describe your project: what you will do, how it addresses the need, who will be involved, timeline.

required_sections.name: Community Support
required_sections.guidance: Show community backing: letters of support, surveys, meetings, partnerships. How do you know the community wants this?

required_sections.name: Budget
required_sections.guidance: Line-item budget. Show how funds will be used. Include any matching funds or in-kind contributions.

required_sections.name: Sustainability
required_sections.guidance: How will this project continue after grant funds are spent? What ongoing resources are needed?

davis_bacon.description: Not typically applicable unless federal construction funds are involved.

section_3.description: Not applicable unless HUD funds are involved.

nepa.description: Not applicable for most community foundation grants.

buy_america.description: Not applicable.

single_audit.description: Not applicable for most community/local grants unless federal funds are involved.

irb.description: Not applicable.

matching.description: Many community grants value matching funds or in-kind support but may not require it.

indirect_cost_rules.notes: Community grants from local foundations typically do not use indirect cost rate structures. Keep overhead minimal and direct most funds to project activities.

submission_portal.name: Various
submission_portal.notes: Community foundations, local government grant programs, and neighborhood development organizations each have their own application processes.

community_project.ai_context: Community project grants emphasize demonstrated community need (with data), community engagement and support, and sustainable impact. Letters of support from community stakeholders are critical. Show how the project addresses an urgent local need and has buy-in from residents. Sustainability plan should explain how benefits continue after the grant period. Budget should maximize direct community benefit.

education.name: Department of Education (ED)

required_sections.name: Project Narrative
required_sections.guidance: Must address absolute, competitive and invitational priorities. Include: (1) need/population, (2) objectives, (3) methods, (4) management plan, (5) evaluation, (6) supporting documents.

required_sections.name: Budget

required_sections.guidance: Justify all costs: personnel (salary, fringe), travel, equipment, supplies, subcontracts, indirect costs. Show calculation methods.

required_sections.name: Management Plan

required_sections.name: Evaluation Plan
required_sections.guidance: Describe evaluation design: logic model, research questions, data collection, analysis methods, how outcomes will be measured.

required_sections.name: Response to Prior Feedback
required_sections.guidance: If you received prior ED award: explain how you addressed any weaknesses identified in prior reviews.

required_documents.name: Logic Model
